name: GSOC inverts feeds into a many-to-one mailbox where anyone with the mined key can write to one node

links.targetTitle: Single Owner Chunks give each Ethereum account a virtual namespace of writable slots
Swarm feeds are sequences of SOCs at predictable addresses that act as mutable pointers

global.title: GSOC inverts feeds into a many-to-one mailbox where anyone with the mined key can write to one node
global.content:
## Claim

**GSOC (Graffiti SOC)** is a many-to-one notification system that inverts the feed model — a shared mined private key lets multiple writers publish SOC updates that a single target node receives in real time.

## Evidence

Mechanics:
- A private key is mined so that its derived SOC address falls in the target node's neighborhood
- Anyone with access to that private key can sign SOC updates
- The target node subscribes via `WS /gsoc/subscribe/{address}` and receives updates as they arrive
- Conceptually a "mailbox" anyone with the key can drop messages into

Comparison to feeds:

|           | Feeds                 | GSOC                              |
|-----------|-----------------------|-----------------------------------|
| Writers   | Single (owner)        | Multiple (anyone with the key)    |
| Readers   | Anyone                | Single target node                |
| Direction | One-to-many broadcast | Many-to-one collection            |

bee-js: `bee.gsocMine(targetOverlay, identifier, proximity?)`, `bee.gsocSend(stamp, signer, identifier, data)`, `bee.gsocSubscribe(address, identifier, handler)`.

## Why it matters

GSOC is the correct primitive for collaboration coordination: collaborators push notifications to a coordinator node without the coordinator needing to poll each participant's feed.
global.description: A private key is mined so its SOC address falls in the target node's neighborhood. Multiple writers share the key; target node subscribes via WebSocket.
